content:
# PPDS Label Template

**Tool ID:** T006-03
**Block:** B006 (PPDS Compliance System)
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Template
**Role:** Kitchen Lead
**Time Required:** 5-10 minutes per label
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. It is designed to be picked up and used immediately, no training required.

A ready-to-use template that removes the guesswork. Under Natasha Law, every PPDS item must carry a label with the product name, full ingredients list with allergens emphasised in bold, and your business name. This template gives you the exact format. Fill it in, print it, and apply it.

**Built for Kitchen Lead working in Food Safety & Hygiene.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Pre-formatted label template meeting Natasha Law requirements, including product name, full ingredients list with allergens emphasised in bold, business name, and date fields. Designed for printing on standard label stock or plain paper with adhesive.

This tool supports **PPDS Compliance System** (B006) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Kitchen Lead (creates labels); All Staff (applies labels to products)
**Time Required:** 5-10 minutes per new label; 1 minute to apply
**Frequency:** Create once per product; update when recipe changes; apply to every PPDS item daily

**What You'll Need:**
- This template (digital version for editing, or pre-printed blanks)
- Allergen Matrix (T005-01) for reference
- Full recipe with exact ingredients for the product
- Supplier spec sheets for any bought-in ingredients
- Label printer or standard printer with adhesive label paper

**Steps:**
1. Identify the product that needs a PPDS label
2. Gather the full recipe and all ingredient lists (including sub-ingredients)
3. Fill in the template below using the exact format shown
4. Cross-reference allergens against T005-01 Allergen Matrix
5. Have a second person verify the allergen information before printing
6. Print and apply to the product before it goes on display
7. File a master copy of each label in your PPDS label folder

---

## Template

### PPDS Label Format

Use this exact format for every PPDS label. The information below is mandatory under Natasha Law.

---

**[PRODUCT NAME]**

**Ingredients:** [Ingredient 1], [ingredient 2], [ingredient 3] (list in descending order of weight, with allergens in **bold**)

**[Your Cafe Name]**
[Your cafe address]

Use by: ___/___/______

---

### Example Completed Labels

#### Example 1: Chicken Pesto Sandwich

---

**Chicken Pesto Sandwich**

**Ingredients:** Sourdough bread (**wheat** flour, water, salt, yeast), chicken breast (chicken, salt, pepper), basil pesto (basil, **cashew nuts**, parmesan cheese (**milk**), olive oil, garlic, salt), rocket, tomato, butter (**milk**)

**Cafe 36**
123 High Street, London N1 1AA

Use by: ___/___/______

---

#### Example 2: Chocolate Brownie

---

**Chocolate Brownie**

**Ingredients:** Dark chocolate (cocoa mass, sugar, cocoa butter, **soya** lecithin), butter (**milk**), sugar, **eggs**, **wheat** flour, cocoa powder, vanilla extract, salt

**Cafe 36**
123 High Street, London N1 1AA

Best before: ___/___/______

---

#### Example 3: Granola Pot with Yoghurt

---

**Granola Pot with Yoghurt**

**Ingredients:** Greek yoghurt (**milk**), granola (**oat** flakes, honey, sunflower oil, **almonds**, pumpkin seeds, coconut flakes), mixed berries (strawberry, blueberry, raspberry)

**Cafe 36**
123 High Street, London N1 1AA

Use by: ___/___/______

---

### Blank Label Template (photocopy and fill in)

---

**______________________________**
*(Product Name)*

**Ingredients:** _______________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________
*(List all ingredients in descending order of weight. Write allergens in BOLD or CAPITALS.)*

**______________________________**
*(Your Cafe Name)*
______________________________
*(Your Cafe Address)*

Use by / Best before: ___/___/______

Prepared by: _____ Date prepared: ___/___/______

---

### Label Formatting Rules

| Rule | Requirement | How |
|------|------------|-----|
| Product name | Must be clear and not misleading | Use the name customers would recognise |
| Ingredients order | Descending order of weight | Largest quantity ingredient first |
| Allergen emphasis | Must be visually distinct from other ingredients | **Bold** is preferred. CAPITALS or underline also acceptable. |
| Sub-ingredients | Must be listed if the component is a compound ingredient | e.g., "pesto (basil, **cashew nuts**, parmesan (**milk**), olive oil)" |
| Business name | Legal name of the business, not just a trading name | Include full address |
| Date marking | Use-by for perishable items; best-before for ambient | High-risk items (sandwiches, dairy) always use "use by" |
| Font size | Minimum 1.2mm x-height (roughly 6pt font) | Ensure legible -- test by reading at arm length |
| Attachment | Label must be attached to the product, not loose | Use adhesive labels or stickers that cannot be easily separated |

### Common Allergen Formatting Reference

When writing ingredients, emphasise these 14 allergens whenever they appear:

| Write This (allergen in bold) | Instead of This |
|-------------------------------|-----------------|
| **wheat** flour | wheat flour |
| **milk**, butter (**milk**) | milk, butter |
| **eggs** | eggs |
| **soya** lecithin | soya lecithin |
| **almonds**, **cashew nuts**, **walnuts** | almonds, cashews, walnuts |
| **peanuts**, **peanut** oil | peanuts, peanut oil |
| **sesame** seeds | sesame seeds |
| **celery** salt | celery salt |
| **mustard** powder | mustard powder |
| contains **sulphites** | contains sulphites |

### Sign-Off

| Role | Name | Signature | Date |
|------|------|-----------|------|
| Label created by (Kitchen Lead) | _____ | _____ | _____ |
| Allergen info verified by (Manager) | _____ | _____ | _____ |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Sub-Ingredients Omitted from the Label

**Why it happens:** The label lists "pesto" as a single ingredient without breaking down its components. Pesto contains tree nuts and milk (parmesan) -- these allergens are hidden if sub-ingredients are not listed.
**How to prevent:** Every compound ingredient must be broken down to its individual components. If pesto is an ingredient, list "pesto (basil, **cashew nuts**, parmesan cheese (**milk**), olive oil, garlic, salt)." Request full ingredient lists from all suppliers.
**If it is already happening:** Pull all labels today. Check every compound ingredient. If any sub-ingredients are missing, rewrite and reprint the label before the next service.

### Risk 2: Labels Printed in Bulk Become Outdated When Recipe Changes

**Why it happens:** The Kitchen Lead prints 200 labels for chicken sandwiches. Three weeks later, the bread supplier changes and the new bread contains sesame. The old labels are still being used because they are pre-printed.
**How to prevent:** Print labels in small batches (one week maximum). When any ingredient or supplier changes, destroy all existing labels for that product and print new ones. Mark the label master file as "UPDATED" with the date.
**If it is already happening:** Audit all pre-printed labels against current recipes and supplier specs. Discard any that do not match. Implement a weekly label review as part of the Monday opening routine.

### Risk 3: Labels Fall Off or Become Illegible

**Why it happens:** Labels are printed on standard paper and stuck with tape. In a fridge environment, condensation makes the tape fail and the ink smudges. The customer receives a product with no readable label.
**How to prevent:** Use adhesive label stock designed for cold/damp environments. Alternatively, use a label printer with thermal or waterproof labels. Test: put a labelled product in the fridge for 4 hours and check the label is still attached and legible.
**If it is already happening:** Switch to better label stock. In the interim, place labels inside clear cellophane wrapping rather than on the outside.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Create a master label for each PPDS product. Store digitally so you can reprint quickly. A simple word processor or label-printing software is sufficient.
**Chain / Franchise:** Your brand may supply centralised label templates or printing systems. Use this tool to supplement or verify those labels.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Even one wrapped item needs a label. Keep a small supply of blank labels and a permanent marker as backup if your printer fails.
**Bakery Cafe:** You will have many PPDS products. Consider investing in a dedicated label printer (Brother, Dymo) that makes daily label production fast and consistent.

**Making it yours:**
- Create a master label file for every PPDS product you sell
- Include your actual cafe name and address on the template
- Store digital versions so any team member can reprint
- Keep blank label stock in the kitchen for emergency relabelling
- Review all labels weekly as part of your allergen management routine

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T006-01 -- PPDS Item Audit Checklist (L1, checklist)
- T006-02 -- Master Ingredients and Allergen Database (L2, tracker)
- T006-04 -- Label Production SOP (L2, SOP)
- T006-05 -- Daily PPDS Label Verification Log (L2, checklist)

**Related blocks:**
- B005

**Parent block:** B006 -- PPDS Compliance System
**Module:** M01: Food Safety & Hygiene Compliance
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
